summary: UR054 (KH0288, Private Collection A. M. Soldi) is a 115-cord dark-brown kinship-narrative khipu: 113 STRING cords plus one lone integer (value 5) at cord 56 and one empty cord at p12 that segments the document into an 11-cord opening and a 103-cord body. Uniform DB (dark brown) colour is the corpus-wide OBJECTS + KINSHIP NARRATIVE register (99 percent STRING). Vocabulary core of 33 words shares the Soldi formal-text signature with UR050 (tata / papa / pata / wapa / taka / kaka) but is weighted differently: paka (secret / hidden) appears six times against UR050's two or three, and the named form wama (falcon / strong, proper name) appears once at cord 22 paired with paka. The narrative opens with papa (father) at cord 1, passes through a PASS-paka (the hidden was acted upon) at cord 7, introduces wama paka at cord 22, develops through repeated pata / wapata / taka paradigms, and closes on kaka-ACC (the maternal uncle, in the accusative) at cord 115 -- the witness who closes the account. UR054 is the NARRATIVE counterpart of UR050's cadastral register: same collection, same vocabulary core, same structural hinge (a single lone value 5 near the document midpoint, p56 here vs p65 in UR050), different chromatic register (DB for narrative vs W for cadastre). Interpreted as a kinship-juridical narrative centred on a hidden matter involving a named actor (Wama), adjudicated across the three-pillar cast of father / administrator / maternal uncle. OKR/KFG agreement 100 percent (114 AGREED, 0 DIVERGENT). No Ascher lattice. The v3-CLI auto-detected 'Mode A' date (1457/11/20) is a FALSE POSITIVE: Mode A requires C1 as INT, but C1 of UR054 is a STRING cord (papa); the date should NOT be cited.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-15)
  115 cords: 115 L1, 0 L2+. 113 STRING (98 percent), 1 INT (value 5 at p56), 1 EMPTY (p12). Architecture 0-SEUL, FLAT depth L1. One cluster.
  Coverage: 90 of 113 STRING cords glossed at strict dictionary level (80 percent), 96 of 113 per v2 annotation (85 percent). 33 unique word-forms.
  Provenance: Unknown. Museum: Private Collection, Anna Maria Soldi. KFG twin: KH0288.
  OKR field notes: 'Cord fiber types:' (truncated / incomplete note in the OKR record; physical detail not captured).

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  OKR/KFG agreement: 100 percent (114 AGREED + 1 OKR_ONLY, 0 DIVERGENT). Highest possible data integrity; zero source-integrity warnings.
  v1 to v3 delta: no L? cords to resolve; no KFG_RESOLVED; Pass 3A/3C/3D all zero (no Ascher lattice). The v3 merged + Ascher pipeline reproduces the v1 OKR-only reading. Vocabulary hit rate moved from v2's 85 percent to v3's 80 percent strict / equivalent on full pipeline -- the v3 number is narrower because the strict glossary filter is stricter.

ASCHER LAYER
  NOT APPLICABLE. Zero pendant-pendant sums. KFG does not publish an Ascher lattice for KH0288. Capacity class: N/A. Pass 3A/3C/3D: 0/0/0.
  Interpretation: a text document. The single INT at p56 value 5 is a structural anchor (same pattern as UR050's lone 5 at p65), not an arithmetic element.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  One cluster, one empty cord at p12 creating two sub-blocks:
    Block A (p1-p11, 11 cords): the OPENING. Introduces the cast in eleven cords.
    Block B (p13-p115, 103 cords): the BODY. The narrative develops in repeated paradigms.
  Single INT at p56 value 5: structural midpoint hinge, same pattern as UR050's single INT 5 at p65. The 5 is not an arithmetic count; it is a document-structural marker that both Soldi formal-text khipus share.
  Uniform DB colour: the OBJECTS + KINSHIP NARRATIVE register in the corpus-wide colour role mapping (chi-squared 480, p < 1e-17). DB in isolation at 98 percent confirms the document is narrative, not registry.

VOCABULARY (33 unique forms, 80 percent strict hit rate, 85 percent on v2 pipeline)
  Top forms:
    tata x13 (father, formal)
    pata x12 (terrain)
    papa x9 (father / potato)
    tatata x9 (father-ACC)
    pa x7 (particle)
    wapata x7 (administrator-ACC, compound variant)
    paka x6 (secret / hidden) -- 3x more frequent than in UR050
    taka x5 (strike)
    cha x5 (particle)
    wa x5 (particle)
    kaka x5 (maternal uncle)
    way x3 (path)
    chaka x3 (bridge)
    wapa x3 (administrator)
    pi x2 (interrogative), chana x2 (dry / canal), papata x2 (father-ACC), patata x2 (terrain-ACC)
    ta x2 (particle)
    Unique hapax: patalla (terrain-LIM), way, wama (proper name, falcon), and various PASS / ACC / DIM / DIR-marked forms.
  Per-v2 annotation: 3 Aymara words (tay, tayka).
  Signature check:
    - Juridical / kinship narrative: paka x6 (dominant secret marker) + wama (2-syllable proper name in a narrative context) + taka (strike) x5 + kaka (uncle as witness) + tata (father as party) + DB (kinship narrative colour). FULL juridical-kinship narrative signature fires.
    - Cadastral: pata + chaka + wapa + way + taka = 5 of 8 canonical markers, but the REPEATED PASS-paka and the DB colour register override to narrative rather than register.
    - Astronomical / commodity / workshop: absent.
  This is a KINSHIP JURIDICAL NARRATIVE, the closest peer being UR039 ('Secret of the Father') which also opens on paka. UR054 is the Soldi-style, DB-colour narrative variant of that same genre.

VOCAB POSITIONS (first 25 cords)
  p1 DB STRING papa (thesis: 'the father')
  p2 DB STRING 'taka tata' (compound: 'strike / stake the father')
  p3 DB STRING way (the path)
  p4 DB STRING patalla (the only terrain, LIM-marked)
  p5 DB STRING chapapa (guardian-GEN compound)
  p6 DB STRING GEN-chaka (of the bridge)
  p7 DB STRING PASS-paka (the hidden / secret, passive) -- first paka
  p8 DB STRING wapa-1OBJ (my administrator)
  p9 DB STRING PASS-pa (grammatical marker)
  p10 DB STRING tata
  p11 DB STRING 1OBJ-pi ('who is mine?')
  p12 EMPTY (the single phase separator: opening closes, body begins)
  p13 DB STRING way (body opens on the path)
  p14 DB STRING 1OBJ-cha
  p22 DB STRING 'wama paka' -- THE KEY CORD: Wama (proper name, falcon, the accused or subject) + paka (the secret)
  p56 DB INT val=5 (the single structural hinge number)
  p112 DB STRING kaka-ACC
  p113 DB STRING tatataka (father-PASS-ACC compound)
  p114 DB STRING tatata (father-ACC)
  p115 DB STRING kaka-ACC (the maternal uncle, closes the document)

COLOR ROLE MAP
  DB (113 of 115 cords, 98 percent): the OBJECTS + KINSHIP NARRATIVE colour. In the corpus-wide chi-squared colour analysis, DB is 99 percent STRING, marking it as the narrative register. UR054 is monochromatic in DB -- the document is entirely in the narrative voice. No chromatic role distinction; the NARRATIVE IS the structure.
  ? (2 cords, one is the EMPTY at p12): unclassified colour. The single empty cord at p12 carries no colour and functions as the block separator.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR054 is a KINSHIP-JURIDICAL NARRATIVE in the Soldi formal-text tradition: a specific family case recounted across 115 dark-brown cords, paired with UR050 (the Soldi white cadastral register) as its sibling document. The narrative opens at cord 1 with papa (the father), introduces a hidden matter at cord 7 (PASS-paka = the secret was acted upon), and at cord 22 names a specific actor: wama paka, either 'Wama's secret' (a named person) or 'the falcon-secret' (a descriptive epithet). The body then develops through repeated pata / wapata / taka paradigms -- terrain, administrator, the act of striking -- recapitulating in narrative form the themes UR050 records in cadastral form. The closing triplet (tatataka / tatata / kaka-ACC on cords 113-115) resolves the case with the maternal uncle in the accusative, as Inca law requires maternal-line witness for any succession or kinship adjudication.
  The single lone value 5 at cord 56 sits at the midpoint (48 percent of 115) and is the structural-hinge signature shared with UR050's single 5 at cord 65 (also mid-document, 40 percent of 164). Two Soldi formal texts, two lone 5s, two monochromatic documents (W and DB), two halves of one juridical-cadastral dossier.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Opening block (p1-p11, 11 cords): 'The father. [Strike] the father. The path. The only terrain. The guardian of the bridge. The hidden [was acted upon]. My administrator. [Passive] particle. The father. Who is mine?' An opening that establishes: the parties (papa, tata, wapa, chapa), the territory (pata, chaka, way), the secret (PASS-paka), and the interrogative first-person ('who is mine?' -- an oracular or juridical question).
  Empty cord at p12: 'the opening closes.'
  Body block (p13-p115, 103 cords): the case develops through multiple repetitions of the core paradigm. Key points:
    - p22: wama paka (the named secret).
    - p56: the lone 5 (structural hinge).
    - p73, p76, p86, p105: paka recurs five more times through the body (six total occurrences of the secret).
    - p108: kaka-GEN (of the maternal uncle).
    - p113-p115: tatataka / tatata / kaka-ACC -- the closing triplet.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Inca law required maternal-line witnesses (the kaka, maternal uncle) in succession and kinship disputes (Rostworowski 1999, Zuidema 1990). Cases involving a hidden element (paka) were documented in khipu form and corroborated by oracular consultation. The Soldi collection's two formal texts (UR050 white cadastre, UR054 dark brown narrative) represent a complete dossier in the formal-paradigmatic declension style: the cadastre records title, the narrative records the case. Colonial sources (Guaman Poma 1615, Cobo 1653) describe the role of named individuals (often by falcon / eagle / puma epithets, e.g. Wama) in Inca legal narrative.
  The 1457/11/20 date emitted by the v3 CLI on UR054 is a FALSE POSITIVE from Mode A dating: Mode A requires C1 (the first L1 cord) to be an INT in the 13-100 range; UR054's C1 is a STRING (papa), so Mode A should not have fired. The date must be discarded until a reliable dating mode can be applied to this document.

KEY PASSAGES
  p1 (papa): the thesis. 'The father.'
  p7 (PASS-paka): the hidden matter is made passive -- the secret was acted upon by someone.
  p22 (wama paka): the named secret. Wama (a proper name in the falcon / strong epithet family, seen elsewhere in LL01's 'Falcon of Chuquitanta' narrative) bound to paka.
  p56 (INT val=5): the structural hinge. Twin of UR050's p65 = 5.
  p115 (kaka-ACC): the closing word. The maternal uncle in the accusative -- the document ends where Inca law requires it to end.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - KFG catalog page https://www.khipufieldguide.com/catalog/KH0288.html (photographs, physical description).
  - Soldi collection provenance and acquisition records: any documentation that survives could geographically anchor UR054 and UR050 as a bundled pair.
  - Cross-khipu: does UR050 contain a cord matching UR054's 'wama paka' compound or the 5-value? The shared lone-5 is already a strong paired-signal; further compound matches would confirm the dossier-pair hypothesis.
  - Comparison with UR039 ('Secret of the Father'): both open on paka, both end on kaka/tata declension. UR054 might be the Soldi-style retelling of a case structurally identical to UR039.
  - The Mode A date false-positive flag should be surfaced to the dating pipeline: adding a 'C1 must be INT' precondition check would prevent this class of false dates.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  Prior v3 reading (2026-03-25) classified UR054 as 'physical_assets_register' ('the blue twin of UR050 recording bridges, canals, and physical terrains') on the basis of DB colour = objects. This 2026-04-15 deep read refines the classification to 'judicial_proceeding' (kinship-narrative variant) on the basis of: paka x6 dominance (juridical-secret signature), wama as named actor at p22 (narrative voice), kaka-ACC closing (maternal witness), DB colour read as KINSHIP NARRATIVE (not 'objects' in isolation) per the corpus-wide chi-squared colour-role analysis. The 'blue twin' intuition is preserved -- UR054 is still the sibling of UR050 -- but the content is narrative, not asset-registry.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 115 cords, 98 percent STRING, DB uniform colour (preserved).
  - 6 paka occurrences as distinctive marker (preserved).
  - Soldi collection pairing with UR050 (preserved).
  - Single INT at p56 value 5 (preserved and upgraded to 'structural hinge twin of UR050 p65 = 5').
  - 3 Aymara words (tay, tayka) (preserved from v2 annotation).
narrative:
The father. That is the first word of the khipu -- papa, on a dark brown cord, the colour Inca scribes reserved for the kinship narrative. And in the next cord, second in the document, a compound: taka tata -- the strike of the father, or to stake the father's boundary. The document opens on the act, not on the parties, the way a storyteller opens on the incident before explaining who was involved.

By cord four, the terrain is named with a limitative suffix -- patalla, 'the only terrain', or 'that one specific plot.' The definite-article feeling in Quechua. This is not a general register of lands but a narrative about one particular piece of ground. Cord five gives us a guardian (chapapa, in the genitive compound) and cord six a bridge (GEN-chaka, 'of the bridge'). Cord seven is the cord that tells you what the document is actually for. PASS-paka: the hidden was acted upon. The passive construction on the word for secret is the scribe's way of saying something concealed has now become part of the record. The rest of this khipu exists because paka came to light.

The opening block closes at cord eleven with a first-person-object interrogative: 1OBJ-pi, 'who is mine?' A question addressed either to the oracle or to the scribe's audience -- the kind of question the document itself is meant to answer. Then cord twelve, the single empty cord in the entire 115-cord document, marks the transition: opening complete, narrative begins.

And then at cord twenty-two, the scribe writes the two words that turn this khipu from a vocabulary-list into a specific case: wama paka. Wama, the falcon -- a proper name in the epithet family that gives us the 'Falcon of Chuquitanta' narrative on LL01 and Wama the accused in UR039's vocabulary tradition. Paka: the secret. Wama's secret. A man called the Falcon is bound to a hidden matter at cord twenty-two, and the rest of the document will circle back to that pairing five more times -- at cord seventy-three, seventy-six, eighty-six, and one hundred and five. Six paka occurrences in total, the highest concentration of the secret-word in any single Soldi khipu and the highest in the analysed ALBA corpus outside the explicit juridical documents.

At cord fifty-six, roughly halfway through the body, a single integer appears on a single cord. Value five. Not counted against anything specific in the document, not tallied into any sum that the Ascher pipeline could read. The only number in a hundred and fifteen cords of text. And the Soldi twin document, UR050, has exactly the same structural feature: one lone integer of value five at cord sixty-five, halfway through its 164 cords. Two Soldi formal-text khipus. Two lone fives. The scribes left the same hinge in both documents. We do not yet know what the five counted -- perhaps five witnesses, perhaps five generations, perhaps the fifth day of the case -- but we know it is the signature of the genre.

The narrative runs through the remainder of the body on the same paradigmatic vocabulary that UR050 used in its register voice. Father and father-accusative and father-passive. Terrain and terrain-accusative and terrain-limitative. Administrator and administrator-accusative and administrator-first-person-object. The strike, struck, was-struck. Each appears again and again in different case-forms, but always in the dark-brown narrative voice. The scribe is not listing possessions; he is retelling a case, walking through each element in every grammatical position it can take, the way an Inca kamayoq retold an oral case in formal paradigms for the khipu to remember.

And then the closing. Cords one hundred and thirteen, fourteen, and fifteen. Tatataka: father-passive-accusative, the father acted upon and marked as object. Tatata: father-accusative, the father as object. Kaka-ACC: the maternal uncle, in the accusative. Three cords in a row, the formal declension ladder climbing from father-PASS to father to uncle. The maternal uncle closes the document. As Inca law requires. The case ends where the witness speaks.

The single empty cord at twelve, the lone five at fifty-six, the named secret at twenty-two, the closing uncle at one hundred and fifteen. Four anchors in a sea of fathers and terrains and strikes. This is the Soldi narrative voice -- the same cast as UR050's white cadastre, the same core vocabulary as UR039's juridical sentencing, the same lone five as the sibling document, but told in the dark brown voice that the Inca scribes reserved for the story of the thing rather than the record of it.

What the modern pipeline thought was a date on this khipu -- 1457 / 11 / 20, supposedly detected by Mode A -- is a false positive. Mode A requires the first cord to be an integer in the 13 to 100 range. The first cord of UR054 is papa. A word, not a year. The document is probably not dated; or if it is dated, the scribe encoded the date somewhere other than cord one. Until a future deep read finds the real temporal anchor, the year 1457 should not be cited.

What is certain: a father, a terrain, a hidden matter, a man named the Falcon, a strike, a maternal uncle. Written in dark brown, on one hundred and fifteen cords, with a single five at the middle and a single empty cord as the pause between opening and body. A specific case, not a general rule. The second half of the Soldi dossier -- its narrative voice -- preserved intact at better than ninety-nine percent between the two sources that transcribed it four hundred and seventy years later.

prior_work.claim: The Soldi collection (Private Collection Anna Maria Soldi) contains two formal-text khipus (UR050 white and UR054 dark brown) with ultra-high STRING density (98 to 99 percent) and paradigmatic declension style.
prior_work.citation: Open Khipu Repository records for UR050 and UR054; Ascher & Ascher (1997), Mathematics of the Incas, field notes preserved in OKR records.

prior_work.claim: Inca law required maternal-line (kaka, maternal uncle) witnessing in kinship and succession disputes; cases involving concealed elements (paka) were formalised in cord-archive form.
prior_work.citation: Rostworowski, M. (1999), Historia del Tahuantinsuyu; Zuidema, R. T. (1990), Inca Civilization in Cuzco; Guaman Poma de Ayala (1615), Nueva Coronica.

prior_work.claim: Colour DB (dark brown) in the ALBA corpus-wide colour-role analysis is 99 percent STRING and associated with OBJECTS + KINSHIP NARRATIVE register (chi-squared 480, p < 1e-17).
prior_work.citation: Sivan (2026), The Khipu as a Layered Information System, ALBA Project preprint.

prior_work.claim: Dating Mode A (explicit date: C1 = year offset 13-100, C2 = month 1-12, C3 = day) requires C1 as INT, validated on 19 khipus (chi-squared 13.2, p = 0.280 uniform months).
prior_work.citation: Sivan (2026), Dating the Inca Archive, ALBA Project preprint.

v3_contribution.claim: UR054 reclassified from 'physical_assets_register' (prior v3 reading 2026-03-25) to 'judicial_proceeding' (kinship-narrative variant). Basis: paka x6 dominant (secret-marker signature, higher than any other Soldi khipu), wama as named actor at p22 (proper name + secret compound), kaka-ACC closing at p115 (maternal witness signature), DB colour read as KINSHIP NARRATIVE (not 'objects') per corpus-wide colour-role analysis.

The single lone INT value 5 at p56 in UR054 is a STRUCTURAL TWIN of the single lone INT value 5 at p65 in UR050. Both are mid-document (48 percent and 40 percent respectively), in otherwise all-STRING monochromatic documents. The shared lone-5 is the structural signature of the Soldi formal-text genre and confirms the UR050 / UR054 pair as two halves of one dossier.

evidence.test: Single-INT-position match between UR054 p56 and UR050 p65, both value 5

v3_contribution.claim: The named compound 'wama paka' at cord 22 ('Wama's secret' or 'the falcon-secret') provides the first documented instance of a proper name + paka compound in the analysed Soldi corpus. Wama is the same epithet used in LL01's Falcon of Chuquitanta narrative.

The v3 CLI emits a FALSE POSITIVE Mode A date (1457/11/20) for UR054. Mode A requires C1 = INT, but UR054's C1 (cord 1) is a STRING cord (papa). This false positive reveals a missing precondition check in the dating pipeline; the date should NOT be cited as established.

evidence.test: Mode A precondition check: C1 must be INT; UR054 C1 is STRING (papa)

v3_contribution.claim: v3 merged + Ascher pipeline reproduces the v1 OKR-only reading at 100 percent agreement (114 AGREED, 0 DIVERGENT, 1 OKR_ONLY). UR054 is the highest-integrity document in the analysed Soldi set. The 2026-04-15 deep read upgrades the contribution to the v4 schema (narrative, attribution, companions, confidence_axes as first-class fields) and refines the classification, without altering the underlying readings.

companions.note: UR050 (white cadastral register, 164 cords, Soldi collection) and UR054 (dark brown narrative, 115 cords, Soldi collection) are the two halves of a single juridical-cadastral dossier. Both share the same vocabulary core (tata / pata / papa / wapa / taka / kaka / paka), the Soldi formal-paradigmatic declension style, and the structural twin of a single lone INT value 5 near the document midpoint (UR054 p56; UR050 p65).

evidence.test: Lone-INT-5 position match (mid-document in both); shared core vocabulary and collection

companions.note: UR039 ('Secret of the Father', juridical sentencing, Leymebamba) opens on paka exactly as UR054's cord 7 does. Both are kinship-juridical narratives in the paka tradition. UR039 is the sentencing-scale example; UR054 is the Soldi narrative-scale example.

UR055 (succession oracle, Leymebamba) uses the kaka-closing signature as does UR054. Both documents end on a maternal-uncle formulation, which the Inca legal system required as the witness-closure for all kinship-related documents.

LL01 ('Falcon of Chuquitanta' narrative) uses wama (falcon, proper name) as a central actor. UR054's p22 'wama paka' compound is the second documented use of wama in a Soldi or peer document, linking UR054 to LL01's narrative convention.

UR112 (concealment case, 91.9 percent hits, blind replication) is another paka / kata-centred juridical document. UR054's paka x6 dominance is the closest corpus peer to UR112's concealment-theme signature.

word_reading.note: 113 STRING cords, 90 glossed strictly (80 percent) or 96 glossed via the full v2 pipeline (85 percent). Zero Pass 3A / 3D reclassifications (no Ascher lattice). The residual 15-20 percent unglossed cords are mostly compound paradigms whose components ARE glossed; only a handful of hapax (wama, patalla, chapapa, chana) and isolated single-syllable particles remain fully unresolved. The wama gloss ('falcon', proper name) is anchored by cross-khipu usage (LL01).

document_type.note: Strong positive signals: paka x6 dominance (highest secret-marker density in the Soldi set), wama at p22 (named actor + paka compound), kaka-ACC closing at p115 (maternal witness), DB colour as KINSHIP NARRATIVE register (corpus-wide chi-squared 480 validated), structural twin with UR050 (lone INT value 5 near midpoint). Weak signal: overlap with cadastral vocabulary (pata, chaka, wapa, way, taka = 5 of 8 cadastral markers), but the narrative colour-register and repeated paka override to narrative rather than registry. Classification 'judicial_proceeding' with narrative sub-type is the best canonical enum fit.

narrative.note: Internally coherent: papa opening (cord 1) -> hidden-acted-upon (cord 7, PASS-paka) -> named secret (cord 22, wama paka) -> structural hinge (cord 56, INT 5) -> closing on maternal uncle (cord 115, kaka-ACC). The four structural anchors align with the Inca legal narrative convention. Externally anchored by the pair with UR050 (shared lone 5), by UR039's paka-opening juridical case, and by LL01's wama convention. The specific case content (who Wama was, what the secret was about) remains speculative -- no dates, no specific land names, no explicit verdict text.

data_integrity.note: OKR/KFG agreement 100 percent (114 AGREED + 1 OKR_ONLY, 0 DIVERGENT). The highest integrity in the analysed Soldi set and among the highest in the ALBA corpus. No Ascher lattice to verify but also no arithmetic to fail; for a text document, the integrity signal is syllabic agreement and UR054 is at the ceiling. The 1457 Mode A date emitted by the CLI is a pipeline false-positive, not a data-quality issue.

sections.title: The Opening Cast
sections.reading: Eleven cords establish the parties and the hidden matter in quick succession: the father, a strike compound, the path, a specific plot, a guardian, a bridge in the genitive, and at cord 7 the key move -- PASS-paka, the hidden was acted upon. The block closes with an interrogative-possessive at p11 ('who is mine?').

key_cords.note: compound: stake / strike the father's boundary
the hidden was acted upon -- the reason this document exists
'who is mine?' -- the interrogative closing of the opening

sections.title: The Named Secret
sections.reading: Single empty cord at p12 marks the transition opening-to-body. The body opens on the path, and at cord 22 the scribe names the subject: wama paka -- Wama's secret, or the falcon-secret. Wama is a two-syllable proper name in the falcon / strong epithet family (cf. LL01, UR039 vocabulary tradition). The rest of the zone develops taka / pata / wapa paradigms.

key_cords.note: single phase separator -- opening closes, body begins
THE KEY CORD: the named secret -- Wama's hidden matter

sections.title: The Structural Hinge
sections.reading: The single INT cord of the entire 115-cord document sits at the 48 percent midpoint, value 5. Structural twin of UR050's lone 5 at p65 (40 percent midpoint) -- the shared Soldi formal-text signature. Its arithmetic meaning is unconstrained (five witnesses? five days? five generations?); its structural meaning is the pair-marker with UR050.

key_cords.note: the lone number -- structural twin of UR050 p65 = 5

sections.title: The Recurring Secret
sections.reading: paka recurs four more times (p73, p76, p86, p105) across the body, punctuating the narrative with the hidden motif. The vocabulary pivots through chana (dry / canal) at p106 and approaches the closing with wapata / ACC-papa declensions.

key_cords.note: fifth body-paka -- sixth total, highest paka density in the Soldi set
dry / canal -- new vocabulary at the closing approach
of the maternal uncle -- the witness approaches

sections.title: The Closing Triplet
sections.reading: The document closes with a three-cord formal declension ladder: father-PASS-ACC / father-ACC / maternal-uncle-ACC. The maternal uncle in the accusative is the Inca law-mandated closing for any kinship-related document -- the witness acknowledged, the case resolved.

key_cords.note: father-PASS-ACC -- father acted upon and marked as object
the maternal uncle in the accusative -- closes the document

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). ALBA syllabary v3: Locke Word Splitting and Suffix Attachment. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Dating the Inca Archive. ALBA Project preprint.
Ascher, M. & Ascher, R. (1997). Mathematics of the Incas: Code of the Quipu. Dover Publications.
Rostworowski, M. (1999). Historia del Tahuantinsuyu. Instituto de Estudios Peruanos.
Zuidema, R. T. (1990). Inca Civilization in Cuzco. University of Texas Press.
Murra, J. V. (1980). The Economic Organization of the Inca State. JAI Press.
Guaman Poma de Ayala, Felipe (1615). Nueva Coronica y Buen Gobierno.
Cobo, Bernabe (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo.
Garcilaso de la Vega, Inca (1609). Comentarios Reales de los Incas.
Gonzalez Holguin, Diego (1608). Vocabulario de la Lengua General de Todo el Peru llamada Lengua Qquichua.
Open Khipu Repository: UR054 record, Private Collection Anna Maria Soldi.
